Title: Police tracking man linked to viral travel scam case
Article:
SHAH ALAM: Police are tracking down an individual known as "Imran" to assist in investigations into an alleged travel service scam that has gone viral on social media.
Selangor police chief Datuk Shazeli Kahar said a report had been lodged in the state, prompting investigations under Section 420 of the Penal Code for cheating.
"We have received a report in Selangor and an investigation paper has been opened under Section 420 of the Penal Code.
"At the same time, we are working to complete the investigation, including efforts to locate the individual," he told reporters at the Selangor police headquarters today.
Shazeli said the Commercial Crime Investigation Department was actively gathering information on the case, adding that the suspect had already been identified.
"The individual's details have been established and the CCID is working to complete the investigation," he said.
He added that police had also received information suggesting the case might involve several states, requiring a wider probe.
"We have received reports and information suggesting involvement across several other states as well," he said.
In a separate development, Shazeli declined to comment on another report involving alleged identity misuse by a former partner, citing ongoing investigations involving multiple police contingents.
"It would not be appropriate for me to comment further at this stage as investigations are still ongoing and involve several contingents," he said.
Earlier, it was reported that a woman had claimed her identity was being used by her ex-husband for debt-related matters without her knowledge.
The case involving an individual known as Imran Koyube has drawn attention following viral social media posts alleging he had previously been involved in similar scams before resurfacing under different identities and agency names.
According to the posts, the suspect is believed to have targeted customers seeking travel services, with several victims reportedly affected.
One claim alleged that a victim's friend was forced to sell jewellery to refund customers after becoming entangled in dealings with the agency.
The posts also alleged that the suspect used his religious background to gain the trust of victims.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Platform barriers, warning systems under consideration for LRT stations
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The government is considering upgrading safety features at Light Rail Transit (LRT) stations, including platform barriers and other warning systems, following recent incidents involving passengers falling onto the tracks.
Transport Minister Anthony Loke said discussions had been held with LRT operator Prasarana Malaysia Bhd, which has proposed installing platform screen doors at stations to enhance passenger safety.
"So what we have done is hold discussions with Prasarana, and they have already put forward proposals to install platform screen doors at our stations.
"So we are now looking at the most practical way to install barriers to provide warnings and improve passenger safety," he told reporters after a road safety programme at Tunku Abdul Rahman University of Management and Technology (TAR UMT).
His remarks follow a recent incident in which a 39-year-old woman was injured at the Abdullah Hukum LRT station in Bangsar after she
fell onto the tracks
and was trapped beneath a train.
The woman sustained injuries to her arm and face, and the incident caused temporary disruption along the Kelana Jaya Line.
Loke said the installation of full platform screen doors was not a straightforward undertaking, as it involves significant costs and extensive modifications, particularly at older stations not originally designed for such systems.
"The installation of platform barriers is not an easy project as it involves high costs.
"All stations would need to be modified because older designs did not originally include them," he said.
He added that newer systems such as the MRT already had the platform screen doors in place, while older LRT lines and the monorail were initially built without such features.
However, Loke said that the platform screen doors had since been installed on the monorail.
He said the government was also considering alternative safety technologies, including sirens, infrared sensors and other alert systems to warn passengers of platform hazards.
"There are various technologies available, not only doors or barriers, but also systems such as sirens and infrared sensors to alert passengers," he said.
He added that Prasarana was currently studying the proposals, with the government hoping the upgrades could be implemented as soon as possible to prevent similar incidents.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 'We're not being wasteful': PN defends motorcycle convoy as fundraiser, road safety drive
Article:
KOTA BARU: Perikatan Nasional (PN) has defended the motorcycle convoy it recently organised, which saw four menteris besar from Pas-led states participate, saying it was not wasteful amid the ongoing energy crisis.
PN secretary-general Datuk Seri Takiyuddin Hassan said the convoy was an initiative to promote responsible and safe motorcycle riding.
"There is no connection to claims of fuel wastage. The programme carried a positive message and should be seen in that context," he said.
"On the same day, we also organised a fundraiser for victims of drink-driving to show our stance against driving under the influence of alcohol," said the Kota Bharu MP.
Takiyuddin added that the event should not be misconstrued, as it was not intended to contradict efforts related to energy conservation.
PKR deputy president
Nurul Izzah Anwar recently criticised
PN's motorcycle convoy as tone-deaf and wasteful amid the energy crisis.
She said that as the world grapples with fragile energy supply chains due to escalating conflict in the Middle East, governments are responding by tightening consumption, building buffers and preparing their economies for prolonged uncertainty.
Izzah added that at a time when Malaysians are adjusting to rising costs and the government is stretching public funds through subsidies, such actions send the wrong message that restraint is optional and responsibility is selective.
PN last Saturday held a motorcycle rally from Dataran Batu Buruk to the Terengganu State Sports Complex in Kuala Terengganu, involving four menteris besar from opposition-led states — Perlis, Terengganu, Kedah and Kelantan.
It was held in conjunction with the Fifth International Ummah Unity Assembly and the Malay World 2026 gathering.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Tighter exports, biodiesel demand to support CPO prices
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Crude palm oil (CPO) prices are expected to remain well supported amid tightening regional export supply, as Thailand and Indonesia prioritise domestic biodiesel programmes, alongside weather risks linked to a developing El Niño.
Public Investment Bank Bhd (PublicInvest) said Malaysia's palm oil inventories are likely to edge towards the psychological two million tonne level over the next two months.
Inventories recently recorded their sharpest decline since March 2023, as major consuming countries stepped up stockpiling amid heightened geopolitical tensions.
CPO prices also rallied in March, rising more than 19 per cent, supported by higher crude oil prices and rising freight costs following the escalation of Middle East conflicts.
These factors have boosted the appeal of biodiesel as a more secure alternative fuel, lifting demand for palm-based biofuels.
PublicInvest maintained its "Overweight" call on the plantation sector and reiterated its full-year CPO price forecast of RM4,400 per tonne.
On regional developments, Thailand is tightening palm oil exports to safeguard domestic supply.
The world's third-largest palm oil producer is projected to produce about 21.8 million tonnes of fresh fruit bunches, equivalent to 3.9 million tonnes of CPO, accounting for around 4.6 per cent of global output.
PublicInvest said the export curbs are aimed at building inventories for biodiesel production and stabilising domestic cooking oil prices amid intensifying geopolitical tensions.
The move is expected to further tighten regional export availability, especially as Indonesia continues to prioritise palm-based biodiesel for domestic consumption.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Three Myanmar nationals remanded further 7 days in countryman's murder case
Article:
BUKIT MERTAJAM: Three Myanmar nationals, including a woman, have been remanded for a further seven days to assist in the investigation into the murder of their countryman on April 5.
Magistrate Savinder Singh Jugindar Singh issued the extension order at the magistrate's court here today to facilitate further probes under Section 302 of the Penal Code.
The suspects — two men aged 37 and 38, and a 37-year-old woman — arrived at the court at 8.30am under police escort. Their previous remand period ended today.
It was earlier reported that a 53-year-old Myanmar factory worker died after he was believed to have been
stabbed
by an acquaintance along Lorong IKS Juru 5 in Simpang Ampat.
Initial investigations found the victim lying face down by the roadside with stab wounds to the ribs. A knife believed to have been used in the incident was recovered near the scene.
Penang police chief Datuk Azizee Ismail confirmed that the three individuals were arrested to assist in the case.
He said a post-mortem conducted at Hospital Seberang Jaya found the cause of death to be a stab wound to the heart.
It is understood that the two male suspects are acquaintances of the main suspect, who is still at large, while the woman is believed to be his girlfriend.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Two feared trapped as fire guts George Town shop house
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: Two people are believed to be trapped in a fire that engulfed a double-storey shophouse along King Street here today.
Penang Fire and Rescue Department assistant operations director John Sagun Francis said they received a distress call at 12.14pm.
"The fire involved a two-storey shophouse unit used as a legal office, measuring about 20 by 80 sq ft.
"About 70 per cent of the premises has been destroyed," he said.
Firefighters from the Lebuh Pantai Fire and Rescue Station, with assistance from the Jalan Perak station, arrived at the scene at 12.25pm.
Efforts to bring the blaze under control were still ongoing at press time.
John said initial reports indicated that two victims were trapped in the building, although their condition had yet to be confirmed.
A total of 12 personnel, including a supervising officer, were deployed to the scene, supported by two fire engines.
Police personnel were also present to assist with traffic and crowd control.
Firefighters reportedly encountered traffic congestion en route to the scene, which may have affected response time.
The situation remains developing.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Sime Darby Property sees strong take-up at Elmina park
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Sime Darby Property Bhd said its latest industrial development, Elmina Business Park 2, has seen encouraging demand since its launch in January, marking its expansion into Rawang in the northern Klang Valley.
The first phases of the project, 1A and 1B, comprising 19 freehold industrial land lots, have achieved a take-up rate of about 70 per cent, reflecting sustained investor and business interest.
Located about two kilometres from the existing Elmina Business Park, the 242.81-hactare (ha) development has a gross development value of RM1.72 billion.
Elmina Business Park 2 will feature 72 units of semi-detached factories, alongside industrial lots ranging from two to four acres, with flexible built-to-suit and built-to-lease options.
The development sits within the Guthrie Corridor, with access to the North-South Expressway and the Kuala Lumpur-Kuala Selangor Expressway via the Guthrie Corridor Expressway.
Chief operating officer for township development Appollo Leong said the company aims to replicate the success of its flagship industrial hub by creating a comprehensive ecosystem for businesses.
"With Elmina Business Park 2 in Rawang, we are delivering the same world-class standards and freehold advantage in a strategically located, high-growth corridor.
"The limited units remaining present a timely opportunity for businesses to secure land in a development that supports long-term growth, operational efficiency and workforce wellbeing," he said in a statement.
The broader Elmina Business Park, spanning 607.03ha, is arguably the Klang Valley's largest freehold industrial business park.
The park achieved full take-up for its signature collection twin factories and secured Google's first hyperscale data centre in the area, which is slated for completion in the second quarter of 2026.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia leads Asia in legacy planning among wealthy
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia is emerging as one of the more proactive markets in Asia for legacy planning among high net worth individuals, according to HSBC Life's inaugural survey covering Asia and the Middle East.
The report, titled "Bridging the intentions-action gap", found that 52 per cent of high net worth individuals in Malaysia have a formal legacy plan, above the survey average of 41 per cent.
This places Malaysia ahead of several more developed wealth hubs, including Singapore (45 per cent), Hong Kong (26 per cent) and Taiwan (24 per cent).
The survey also showed that Malaysians tend to start planning earlier than their regional peers.
About 82 per cent of respondents in Malaysia said they began planning before the age of 50, the highest proportion among the nine markets surveyed. In contrast, Taiwan recorded the lowest early-start rate, with 40 per cent only beginning after age 50.
HSBC Life said the findings reflect a broader regional shift as Asia undergoes significant wealth creation and intergenerational transfer.
In a statement, HSBC Malaysia country international wealth and premier banking head Linda Yip said the region is in the midst of one of the largest wealth transfers in its history, with Malaysia firmly part of the trend.
She said Malaysia's economic expansion, including gross domestic product growth of 5.2 per cent in 2025, alongside rising incomes, is driving the emergence of more entrepreneurial wealth and a growing base of younger high net worth individuals.
Yip added this is reflected in Malaysians' approach to legacy planning, with more individuals putting formal plans in place and starting earlier.
She said family considerations remain a key concern, with 43 per cent of respondents citing the potential for family disputes, underscoring the need for clear planning.
"While Malaysia is making strong progress, the survey also highlights an opportunity to broaden the solutions clients use.
"Across the nine markets surveyed, 25 per cent of high net worth individuals use life insurance as their main legacy planning tool, while Malaysia stands at 17 per cent.
"Life insurance can complement other arrangements by helping families protect beneficiaries and transfer wealth in a more structured and predictable way," she added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] Job scam call centre targeting Japan busted in Seri Kembangan, nine held
Article:
SHAH ALAM: Police have crippled a job scam syndicate operating a call centre from a residence Seri Kembangan, Selangor, following a raid on April 8.
Selangor police chief Datuk Shazeli Kahar said the operation was carried out by the Serdang Commercial Crime Investigation Department (CCID) with assistance from social media analysts from the Petaling Jaya police.
"A total of nine suspects, aged between 22 and 44, were arrested. They comprise one local man, three women and five foreign men," he told reporters at the Selangor police headquarters today.
Police also seized six computers, 16 mobile phones, five modems and a set of house keys with a remote control.
He said investigations revealed the syndicate was involved in online job scams targeting victims in Japan.
"The group used messaging applications such as LINE and Telegram to create fake accounts using fabricated identities to gain victims' trust.
"They advertised part-time job offers on social media platforms to lure victims. Those who responded would be contacted and asked to provide personal details, including phone numbers and social media accounts," Shazeli said.
He added that each syndicate member had specific roles, including promoting advertisements, communicating with victims and handling financial transactions, while internal communications were conducted via Telegram.
Police believe the syndicate had been operating for about two months using local internet networks.
"Initial remand orders were obtained for four days from April 9 to April 12 at the Petaling Jaya magistrate's court.
"However, a subsequent application for further remand was rejected, and one suspect has since been released on police bail," he said, adding that the remaining eight foreign suspects have been placed under immigration detention from April 13 to April 26 to assist further investigations.
Shazeli said the case is being investigated under Section 420 of the Penal Code for cheating and Section 120B for criminal conspiracy.
Members of the public with information are urged to contact the Serdang district police hotline at 03-8074 2222.
Shazeli also advised the public to remain vigilant and not be easily deceived by suspicious online job offers.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: RM4.2mil in drugs seized at KLIA, 4 arrested
Article:
SHAH ALAM: Police have dismantled two drug trafficking syndicates at Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA), seizing narcotics worth more than RM4.2 million and arresting four suspects in a series of operations earlier this month.
Selangor police chief Datuk Shazeli Kahar said the largest seizure involved 45kg of cannabis valued at RM3.6 million, believed to have been destined for the international market, particularly Europe.
"The drugs were detected on April 6 when Malaysia Airports Holdings Berhad auxiliary police intercepted three suspicious pieces of luggage.
"A subsequent inspection by officers from the KLIA Narcotics Criminal Investigation Division uncovered 90 packets of compressed cannabis concealed within the bags.
"All three bags belonged to local men who were scheduled to board an international flight," he told reporters at the Selangor police headquarters here today.
MORE TO COME
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Lorry driver charged with parang attack in Bera
Article:
TEMERLOH: A lorry driver was charged at the Sessions Court here today with slashing a man with a parang in Bera last month.
A. Premanandh, 39, claimed trial after the charge was read to him in Tamil before judge Jamaludin Mat.
The accused is alleged to have intentionally caused grievous hurt to the 35-year-old victim using a weapon capable of causing death.
The offence was allegedly committed on the shoulder of Jalan Besar Mengkuang in Triang, Bera, at 9.16pm on March 29.
He was charged under Section 326 of the Penal Code for causing grievous hurt with a dangerous weapon, which carries a penalty of up to 20 years' imprisonment, a fine, or whipping upon conviction.
Deputy public prosecutor Khairunnisa Zaffar proposed bail of RM8,000 with one surety, informing the court that the accused has a prior record related to drug offences.
In mitigation, lawyer C. Varun pleaded for lower bail, saying his client, the sole breadwinner earning RM1,800 monthly, supports his wife, a school-going child, his parents, and his sister.
He said that the family could not afford the amount proposed by the prosecution, and they could raise RM5,000.
When asked about the victim's condition, Khairunnisa said the man sustained deep cuts to his hands and that the two men were known to each other.
Jamaludin set bail at RM5,000 with one surety and fixed May 18 for mention.
He also imposed additional conditions, requiring the accused to report to the nearest police station once a month and to refrain from contacting prosecution witnesses, including the victim, until the case concludes.
It was previously reported that the victim was knocked unconscious during the assault, which went viral after occurring near a restaurant in Triang.
Police launched a
manhunt
and arrested several individuals to assist in investigations last week.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] Police hunt woman who abandoned RM1.2mil drugs at KLIA
Article:
SHAH ALAM: Police are hunting a woman who fled the Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) after abandoning a bag containing more than RM1.2 million worth of methamphetamine.
Selangor police chief Datuk Shazeli Kahar said the drugs were detected on March 30 during routine baggage scanning by Malaysia Airports Holdings Bhd auxiliary police.
A subsequent inspection uncovered 23 packets of suspected syabu weighing approximately 24.5 kilogrammes.
"The bag's owner was called for verification, but upon realising the luggage had been flagged, the suspect fled the terminal and did not board her flight," he told reporters at the Selangor police headquarters here today.
Police have identified the suspect as Syazwani Farhana Dron, from Kampung Tudan, Lutong in Miri, Sarawak, and have listed her as a wanted individual.
"The drugs, estimated to be worth RM1.22 million, were believed to be intended for distribution in Sabah.
"Authorities are actively tracking the suspect and have urged members of the public with information on her whereabouts to come forward," he said.
Shazeli added that the case is being investigated under Section 39B of the Dangerous Drugs Act 1952.
Those with information can contact Inspector Rohaya Razali at 019-768 6165, the Selangor NCID hotline at 03-5514 5308 or the Selangor police operations room at 03-5520 2222.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Syed Hamid Albar mulls political comeback to Umno
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Former Umno Supreme Council member Tan Sri Syed Hamid Albar says he is "seeking inspiration" on whether to return to Umno after leaving the party in 2018.
Syed Hamid, who later joined Bersatu before announcing his retirement from politics in 2022, described Umno as the best party for Malays compared to others.
"I am still sitting and looking for inspiration. It may take a few days to decide whether to reactivate my membership in Umno or remain retired," he said.
"To me, the only party that has the machinery, grassroots and network for the Malays is Umno," he added.
Previously, Umno president Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said the party is open to any former members who were sacked or suspended to rejoin without conditions.
The deputy prime minister said the invitation under the "Rumah Bangsa" initiative is also open to anyone wishing to join Umno's struggle.
Among those reported to be considering a
return to Umno
are former vice-president Datuk Seri Hishammuddin Hussein, former Youth chief Khairy Jamaluddin, and former Jempol Umno division chief Datuk Seri Mohd Salim Mohd Sharif.
Syed Hamid, who previously served as foreign minister, defence minister and home minister, joined Bersatu in 2018 before being appointed chairman of the party's election committee.
A former Kota Tinggi member of parliament, he said efforts for Umno to move forward should be prioritised as the party already has a strong Malay support base compared to other parties.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] Cops: Possible SOP breach in Sungai Buloh Prison detainee escape
Article:
SHAH ALAM: Police have opened an internal investigation into possible breaches of standard operating procedures (SOP) following the escape of a remand detainee from the Sungai Buloh Prison.
This latest development follows an ongoing
large-scale manhunt
with authorities believing the suspect remains within the district.
Last week, a Pakistani detainee escaped during a transfer at Sungai Buloh Prison after attending court proceedings at the Shah Alam Court on April 9.
Selangor police chief Datuk Shazeli Kahar said a separate investigation paper has been opened under Sections 223 and 224 of the Penal Code to determine whether any negligence or non-compliance led to the incident.
He confirmed that statements have been recorded from multiple parties, including police personnel, prison officers and detainees who witnessed the incident.
"No arrests have been made so far, but officers involved have been reassigned to non-active duties pending the outcome of investigations," he told reporters at the Selangor police headquarters today.
Shazeli declined to disclose the number of personnel under scrutiny, citing ongoing inquiries.
Meanwhile, efforts to track down the escapee have been intensified, with additional manpower deployed from multiple units and agencies.
"We have increased the strength of the search teams, involving various units including the General Operations Force, Air Operations Force and technical units," he said.
While declining to reveal the total number of personnel involved, Shazeli said he is confident the operation would yield results soon.
"We believe the suspect is still within the district. The search perimeter has been widened slightly, but remains focused within the same area."
He added that police have also taken steps to disrupt any potential communication between the escapee and outside contacts.
Video of the
detainee escaping
during a transfer at Sungai Buloh Prison has gone viral.
In the clip shared on X by UpdateInfo, the man is seen alighting from a police truck with several other detainees.
A few seconds later, he is seen sprinting away from the group within the compound, while two policemen attempt to give chase.
The man, Muhammad Hassan, is facing multiple charges, including under Section 377C and Section 392 of the Penal Code, Section 14(a) and Section 14(d) of the Sexual Offences Against Children Act, and Section 15(1)(c) of the Immigration Act.
Section 377C of the Penal Code relates to sexual intercourse against the order of nature committed without consent or involving a person under the age of 18.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Trump rejects Pope Leo's appeal for peace, calls him 'liberal'
Article:
JOINT BASE ANDREWS, Maryland: US President Donald Trump told reporters Sunday that he is "not a big fan" of Pope Leo XIV, after the global leader of Catholics made a plea for peace.
"I'm not a big fan of Pope Leo. He's a very liberal person, and he's a man that doesn't believe in stopping crime," Trump told reporters at Joint Base Andrews in Maryland.
He accused the pontiff of "toying with a country that wants a nuclear weapon."
On Saturday, the 70-year-old American
pope publicly implored leaders
to end the violence, telling worshippers at St. Peter's Basilica: "Enough of the idolatry of self and money! Enough of the display of power! Enough of war!"
Trump reiterated his comments to reporters with a post on Truth Social saying: "I don't want a Pope who think it's OK for Iran to have a Nuclear Weapon."
Washington and the Vatican have recently denied reports of a rift.
On Friday, a Vatican official denied reports that a top Pentagon official gave the church's envoy to the United States a "bitter lecture" over Pope Leo's criticisms of the Trump administration.
The story in the Free Press – which the Pentagon had already dismissed as "distorted" – reported that Cardinal Christophe Pierre was summoned in January to the Pentagon, where he was given a dressing-down by US Under Secretary of Defense for Policy Elbridge Colby.
The military official reportedly told the cardinal that the United States "has the military power to do whatever it wants – and that the Church had better take its side."
Vatican spokesman Matteo Bruni said in a statement "the account presented by certain media outlets regarding this meeting does not correspond to the truth in any way."
While both parties insist the meeting was cordial, the Holy See and the White House have openly been at odds over the Trump administration's hardline mass deportation campaign – which the pope called "inhuman" – and the use of military force in the Middle East and Venezuela.
When Trump made genocidal threats against Iran Tuesday – saying "A whole civilisation will die tonight, never to be brought back again" – the pontiff slammed the "truly unacceptable" statement and urged parties to "come back to the table" for negotiations.
Earlier this month, Pope Leo hailed the news of a ceasefire between the United States and Iran as a "sign of real hope."
But peace talks between the United States and Iran, held in the Pakistani capital Islamabad, ended abruptly Saturday with US Vice President JD Vance telling reporters after a marathon-session of talks that Washington has delivered its "final and best offer."--AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] Probe into 12 Kajang police personnel ongoing, awaiting technical reports
Article:
SHAH ALAM: Investigations into 12 police personnel arrested in Kajang over a cryptocurrency robbery are nearing completion, but remain pending due to outstanding technical reports.
Selangor police chief Datuk Shazeli Kahar said the case has yet to be referred to the deputy public prosecutor as authorities are awaiting input from relevant technical agencies, particularly those involving cryptocurrency transactions.
"The investigation is almost complete, but we are still waiting for several technical reports, especially those related to cryptocurrency transactions," he told reporters at the Selangor police headquarters today.
He declined to name the agencies involved, describing them only as technical entities assisting in the probe.
Shazeli added that all 12 personnel are still on duty but have been reassigned to non-operational roles, including administrative tasks.
"They are still serving, but not in frontline duties while investigations are ongoing," he said.
Last month, it was reported that investigations into the alleged gang robbery involving 12 policemen are still ongoing, with Selangor police awaiting legal advice and digital
forensic findings
before completing the probe.
The investigation papers had been referred to the police legal division, while authorities were still awaiting the digital forensic report.
Selangor police had earlier confirmed the
arrest of 12 officers
over an alleged gang robbery involving eight Chinese nationals who were reportedly forced to transfer 50,000 USDT (about RM200,000) in cryptocurrency.
The officers are being investigated under Sections 395 and 397 of the Penal Code for gang robbery and aggravated robbery.
Police said they were alerted after a report was lodged on Feb 6, adding that swift action led to the arrests.
The case has drawn public attention following claims by the complainant, a 31-year-old Chinese national, that he and several acquaintances were compelled to transfer cryptocurrency after police raided a rented bungalow in Country Heights, Kajang, on suspicion of scam-related activities.
Earlier, a lawyer representing the group questioned the handling of the case, saying his clients had not received any updates two weeks after the alleged incident.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MMEA foils RM1 million syabu smuggling attempt off Kota Tinggi
Article:
KOTA TINGGI: The Malaysian Maritime Enforcement Agency (MMEA) foiled an attempt to smuggle 20kg of syabu worth RM1 million near Batu Layar on April 8.
Tanjung Sedili Maritime Zone acting director Maritime Commander Mohd Najib Sam said a patrol boat intercepted the shipment at 9.40pm about 0.4 nautical miles northwest of Batu Layar.
"Our enforcement officers were on routine duty when they detected a suspicious, unregistered fibreglass boat heading towards a neighbouring country.
"Upon realising MMEA's presence, the skipper acted aggressively by turning back into national waters to evade arrest," he told a press conference today.
Najib said following a 10-minute chase, two men on the boat jumped overboard about 700m from the shore and escaped.
"A search of the abandoned vessel uncovered a bag containing 20 yellow packages filled with suspected syabu. Initial investigations suggest the drugs were intended for delivery to a neighbouring country.
"We do not rule out the possibility that another boat or ship was waiting in foreign waters to collect the drugs, given the suspect's boat was only powered by an 85-horsepower outboard engine.
"The use of an 85hp engine suggests the boat lacked the power and range for a full cross-border run, indicating it was likely used for a short-distance transfer to a larger vessel waiting offshore," he said.
The narcotics and the boat, valued at RM40,000, were taken to the Pengerang maritime post jetty for investigation under the Dangerous Drugs Act 1952.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Supporting farmers an investment in national stability
Article:
LETTERS:
Recent warnings that farmers may stop planting due to rising diesel costs are symptoms of a deeper, longstanding failure in how the country's food system is structured.
If farmers are forced out of production, the consequences will be immediate — higher prices, tighter supply and deeper dependence on imports.
The real issue lies in a system where those who produce food are the least rewarded. The real gains are often captured by input suppliers, intermediaries and large commercial players, while farmers continue to bear the risks, rising costs and physical labour. In simple terms, those who toil get the least, while those removed from production take the biggest share.
At the same time, subsidy policies reflect misplaced priorities. Public resources are not always directed towards sectors that serve the collective good. Instead, support often flows towards areas that do not directly contribute to national food security.
Subsidies must prioritise food production that benefits the many. Supporting farmers is not a cost — it is an investment in national stability. A country that cannot support its own producers is surrendering control over its food system. In a world increasingly shaped by climate disruptions, geopolitical tensions and fragile supply chains, this is a risk Malaysia cannot afford.
Immediate support is needed to stabilise production costs, including ensuring fuel supply for farmers. More importantly, the country must address the deeper structural imbalance that has long disadvantaged those who produce food.
As a voice representing rural consumers and communities, we call for urgent and meaningful reforms to ensure that the nation's food system is fair, resilient and centred on the needs of the rakyat.
M.S. ANUAR MAHMOD
Secretary,
RURAL MALAYSIA – Persatuan Pengguna Luar Bandar & Ekologi Malaysia
The views expressed in this article are the author's own and do not necessarily reflect those of the New Straits Times
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: NexG clarifies losses, says concerns misplaced
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: NexG Bhd said concerns over its financial position, investment decisions and governance practices stem from misinterpretation of information that had already been disclosed to the market.
The company said losses linked to its investment were due to fair value adjustments, which are non-cash accounting entries required under Malaysian Financial Reporting Standards.
These were recognised in its third-quarter results, approved by the board and announced to Bursa Malaysia in line with listing requirements.
"As such, the matter is not new and has already been fully disclosed to the market in a timely and transparent manner," it said in a statement today.
"Accordingly, such losses should not be interpreted as realised losses or indicative of deterioration in the company's underlying business performance."
NexG attributed the valuation impact to temporary uncertainties during the period, particularly over its ability to exercise influence over the investment.
It added that the adjustments were situational and did not reflect the investment's intrinsic long-term value.
On the boardroom tussle, NexG said the issue was resolved following the resignation of several directors who failed to secure sufficient shareholder support ahead of a planned extraordinary general meeting.
It added that the board has since stabilised, with Raya Aviation Holdings Sdn Bhd emerging as the largest shareholder, providing clearer strategic direction and governance stability.
On the "internal review" cited in media reports, NexG said it was conducted to assess reputational impact and stakeholder concerns arising from negative publicity.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Grieving aunt willing to forgive teacher in fatal school car accident
Article:
KOTA BARU: The primary guardian of the 13-year-old student killed after being hit by a car at a secondary school in Pengkalan Chepa yesterday has expressed her willingness to forgive the driver, though the final decision rests with the girl's father.
Zuraifah Noor, 43, who had cared for Nur Fatimatul Hawa Mohd Azaudin for the past five years, said the family remains in shock and is not yet ready to consider legal action.
"As for me, I am willing to forgive. However, I leave it entirely to the victim's father and other family members.
"We are still mourning and unable to think clearly. For now, we are leaving everything to the police and the Education Ministry to carry out investigations," she said when met at Kampung Gayong today.
Zuraifah, who is also the victim's aunt, noted that while the accident occurred in an area where vehicles were not supposed to be, the family accepted it as fate.
"We believe this is determined by Allah. No one intends to hit another person deliberately. What happened was beyond expectation," she said.
Zuraifah added that she briefly met the teacher's family at the district police station yesterday while lodging a report.
"When we met, I wouldn't say I was angry. I just felt empty," she said, adding that the teacher's family had been advised not to visit the victim's home for the time being.
The 57-year-old teacher has been remanded for two days to facilitate investigations under Section 41(1) of the Road Transport Act.
The remand order was issued by magistrate Wan Muhammad Izzat Wan Abdullah today.
In the incident at SMK Tanjong Mas yesterday, the Form One girl died at the scene from severe head injuries, while another student was seriously injured.
Police said the driver is believed to have accidentally pressed the accelerator, causing the vehicle to veer and hit the two students as they were putting on their shoes in front of the school surau.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Inspace Creation designs its way to ACE Market, to debut on May 8
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Interior fitting-out services provider Inspace Creation Bhd has launched its prospectus for an initial public offering (IPO) ahead of its ACE Market debut on Bursa Malaysia on May 8.
The IPO will raise RM17.13 million through the issuance of 68.50 million new ordinary shares at 25 sen each, alongside an offer for sale of 29.30 million existing shares, the company said at the launch today.
Of the proceeds, RM6 million is allocated for capital expenditure, RM4.39 million for working capital, RM2.74 million for bank borrowings repayment, and RM4 million for listing expenses.
Applications for the public issue open today and will close on April 22.
Inspace Creation has evolved into a full-service interior fit-out provider, offering project planning and management, design and build solutions as well as maintenance services.
Executive director Wong Chong Siong said this integrated model, combined with a strong client portfolio, differentiates the company from its peers and strengthens execution capabilities across projects.
He said the IPO will enhance operational capacity, including the establishment of dedicated storage and mock-up facilities to improve procurement efficiency and enable clients to experience design concepts physically.
"Our immediate priority post-listing is to strengthen our corporate positioning by securing larger-scale projects while diversifying into broader commercial segments," he said at a press conference today.
These include collaborations with developers on show units, sales galleries and project head offices, as well as expanding into hospitality fit-outs and selective small-scale construction works.
It is also targeting geographic expansion beyond the Klang Valley, with a focus on Penang and Johor Bahru.
The interior fitting-out industry in Malaysia is expected to grow, with the value of work projected to increase from RM2.8 billion in 2025 to RM3.5 billion in 2027, representing a compound annual growth rate of 11.8 per cent.
Addressing rising material cost, Wong said it remains flexible due to its design-and-build model, allowing it to apply value engineering to optimise material selection and manage client budgets without significantly impacting margins.
The company has completed more than 110 projects with a cumulative contract value exceeding RM170 million, including fit-out works in Tun Razak Exchange and Bukit Bintang, as well as various commercial developments across Selangor and Kuala Lumpur.
Inspace Creation has an order book of RM30.28 million.
Upon listing, the company will have an enlarged share capital of 369.30 million shares and a market capitalisation of RM92.33 million.
TA Securities Holdings Bhd is the principal adviser, sponsor, underwriter and placement agent.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: TNB pushes towards 70pct renewable energy capacity by 2050
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Tenaga Nasional Bhd (TNB) is actively operationalising its roadmap to achieve 70 per cent renewable energy (RE) capacity by 2050, chief sustainability officer Leo Pui Yong said.
This commitment is manifested among others, through a robust pipeline, including a 2.5-gigawatt hydro hybrid floating solar initiative and a 500-megawatt (MW) solar farm development.
As of 2025, RE accounted for approximately 26.5 per cent of TNB's total generation capacity, with a total installed green capacity of 4,303MW.
"To anchor these efforts, TNB's carbon management plan focuses on optimising current generation assets to ensure every megawatt carries a lower emissions footprint, reinforcing a practical and immediate pathway towards sustainability," she said at the Sustainability Week Asia 2026, held recently.
While expanding generation capacity is crucial, grid readiness remains the ultimate enabler.
"Under the Incentive-Based Regulation framework, the current Regulatory Period 4 (RP4) allocates RM43 billion for grid investments, which is more than double the previous cycle."
This unprecedented capital commitment underscores TNB's resolve to build a smarter grid capable of managing increased renewable penetration. To further secure this infrastructure, TNB is launching a Battery Energy Storage System (BESS) pilot to mitigate intermittency and enhance overall grid stability.
TNB's energy evolution also serves as a regional anchor, Leo said.
Existing cross-border links already facilitate security of supply, with 1,000MW of connectivity to Singapore and 380MW to Thailand.
In a major step toward the ASEAN Power Grid (APG), TNB has operationalised ENEGEM (Energy Exchange Malaysia). This platform facilitates up to 50MW of cross-border green electricity trading, transforming regional integration into a functional and agile ecosystem.
TNB has been actively contributing to the APG, as evidenced by cross-border connectivity projects such as the Vietnam–Malaysia–Singapore, Malaysia–Indonesia, and Malaysia–Sarawak interconnections.
The transmission of renewable energy to Singapore from Laos via Thailand and Malaysia three years ago marked a milestone in the region's cross-border electricity trade.
It should be noted that the APG is a regional initiative aimed at integrating the electricity networks of Southeast Asian countries to enhance energy security, optimise resource sharing, and support the transition to sustainable energy across the region.
The initiative facilitates multilateral power trading and strengthens cross-border interconnectivity among ASEAN member states.
As global demand evolves, particularly with the growth of data centre investments, speed of delivery is now as essential as sustainability, Leo said, adding that through the Green Lane pathway, TNB is streamlining infrastructure timelines to enable power delivery within a 12-month window.
"This responsiveness reflects close collaboration with customers to meet rapidly growing large-scale energy demand. From policy clarity to infrastructure readiness, Malaysia's energy transition is moving decisively from a state of ambition to a state of execution," she said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 'Work smart,' Saifuddin tells police on fuel smuggling enforcement
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail has told police to "work smart" by using data-driven methods to identify petrol stations at the borders that are high-risk for fuel smuggling.
He said although it was previously announced that 80 petrol stations would be monitored, the ministry has since doubled that figure to
170 stations located near Malaysian borders
.
Saifuddin said police must work with the Finance Ministry and Domestic Trade Ministry to check sales data for suspicious volumes.
"First, we must define the scope of the task, determine which Acts to apply, and decide which teams to deploy. We must work smart.
"We are not looking to guard all 4,000 petrol stations. We want to guard the 80 plus 90, which is the170 stations located near our borders.
"I suggest obtaining precise data from the Finance and Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministries regarding sales volumes. If a station's sales figures are illogical or sky-high, zoom in on them immediately," he said at the Home Ministry's monthly assembly, adding that this was not usually the police's jobscope.
He added that increasing surveillance at these specific locations is the essence of working smart.
"Data must be shared by the relevant ministries because our role is to assist in enforcement. That is the directive," Saifuddin said.
He added that these heightened measures would remain in place for as long as global conflicts continued to impact Malaysia's economy.
However, he assured that the strategy would be revised once smuggling activities showed a significant decline.
He said the Home Ministry is committed to plugging leakages of subsidised goods during the current crisis to prevent further damage to the national economy.
Saifuddin has also ordered an immediate upgrade of border patrol stations, describing the current state of some facilities as being in "worse condition than a burger stall."
His order followed a personal working visit yesterday to several border points, including Padang Besar, Bukit Kayu Hitam, Durian Burung, and Pangkalan Hulu.
"I went down myself to see the situation. I told the secretary-general (to the Home Ministry) that I cannot bear to see the conditions our personnel are working in.
"The shelters they use look like burger stalls; in fact, some burger stalls look better.
"I have told the secretary-general to squeeze the budget a little to resolve this, particularly in Pangkalan Hulu, so the posts look better.
"These personnel are responsible for controlling traffic and preventing smuggling, yet their rest areas are... Masya-Allah. It is just not conducive," he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysian's second shot at wedded bliss ends in immigration, bigamy bust
Article:
JOHOR BARU: A Malaysian man's plan to remarry in Indonesia has unravelled into a legal predicament after he was detained by immigration authorities for overstaying his visa and allegedly falsifying documents.
Indonesian media reported that Malaysian Mohamad Zukri, 56, was arrested by immigration enforcement officers in Ponorogo, East Java, following checks which found he had remained in the country well beyond his permitted stay.
He is also accused of attempting to marry another Indonesian woman despite still being legally married.
Authorities said Zukri first entered Indonesia in 2014 and married an Indonesian woman in Salatiga, Central Java, but never formally dissolved the marriage. His subsequent attempt to marry again in Pacitan raised suspicions of unlawful polygamy.
The case came to light after the Religious Affairs Office in Pacitan detected inconsistencies in documents submitted for the proposed marriage.
Ponorogo Immigration chief Anggoro Widy Utomo said investigations found that Zukri's passport had expired, constituting a violation of immigration laws.
Further checks revealed a pattern of non-compliance. Zukri had previously overstayed his visa by 56 days in 2014 before being deported to Malaysia.
He later re-entered Indonesia and is believed to have overstayed again since 2018.
Anggoro warned that repeated immigration offences could carry penalties of up to five years' imprisonment and fines of up to Rp500 million (about RM140,000).
He said that Zukri was also suspected of misrepresenting his marital status by declaring himself a widower in official documents, despite still being legally married.
"Foreigners intending to marry Indonesian citizens must provide valid proof of their marital status. In this case, the information provided was false," he added.
Anggoro said that enforcement action was necessary to prevent misuse of marriage procedures as a means of extending stay in Indonesia.
The case comes amid broader scrutiny of marriage regulations in Indonesia, including ongoing debates over polygamy rules for civil servants, which authorities say are aimed at tightening oversight and safeguarding family welfare.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bukit Aman warns of scams targeting victims with fake money recovery offers
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bukit Aman has warned of a new scam tactic targeting previous fraud victims, where syndicates pose as authorities offering to recover lost funds only to deceive them a second time.
Commercial Crime Investigation Department (CCID) director Datuk Rusdi Mohd Isa said the modus operandi typically begins with victims being lured by Facebook advertisements before being contacted via WhatsApp by suspects impersonating National Scam Response Centre (NSRC) representatives.
"The suspects would then claim they could help recover money lost in earlier scams, but would require victims to make upfront payments as part of the so-called service.
"Victims who believe the offer are instructed to make multiple transactions into different bank accounts. However, they are continuously asked to make additional payments without receiving any refunds as promised," he said in a statement today.
Rusdi stressed that no party can guarantee the recovery of scammed funds, especially through services that require advance payments.
He advised the public not to fall for such offers and to always verify with authorities before making any financial transactions.
Based on CCID records, six cases involving such recovery scams were recorded between 2025 and March this year, with total losses amounting to RM68,491.
Rusdi said the cases were reported across several contingents, including Selangor, Kuala Lumpur, Johor and Terengganu, with most victims having been deceived for a second time.
"Members of the public who fall victim to scams are urged to immediately contact the NSRC at 997 or lodge a report at the nearest police station to enable swift action to be taken," he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Senior pathologist tells inquest Zara's injuries consistent with fall from height
Article:
KOTA KINABALU: A senior forensic pathologist has concurred with findings that 13-year-old Zara Qairina Mahathir's fatal injuries were consistent with a fall from height.
Datuk Seri Dr Bhupinder Singh Jeswant Singh, 74, testified before Coroner Amir Shah Amir Hassan today in the inquest into the teenager's death.
Bhupinder, an associate professor in forensic and legal medicine at the Royal College of Surgeons in Ireland & University College Dublin Malaysia Campus (RUMC) in Penang, was the 68th witness called by the legal team led by Datuk Ram Singh.
"I have reviewed all the documents provided to me pertaining to this case.
"A meticulous post-mortem examination was carried out by the forensic pathologist, and I agree with the findings regarding both external and internal injuries," he said when reading his witness statement.
Zara was found unconscious on the ground floor of her hostel and was pronounced dead the next day, July 17, 2025, at Queen Elizabeth Hospital (QEH).
Her remains were exhumed on Aug 9, 2025, with a post-mortem performed the following day at the QEH Department of Forensic Medicine.
Bhupinder told the court he agreed with the opinion of Dr Jessie Hiu, who previously testified that the nature of the injuries was consistent with a fall from height.
Hiu had also testified that based on a scene examination, it was
unlikely
the deceased accidentally fell or was pushed from a standing position next to the concrete railing along the corridor.
However, Bhupinder cautioned that it was unwise to over-interpret the relationship between observed injuries and the estimated height of a fall.
He explained that injury patterns depend on height, body weight, velocity, the nature of the impacted surface, body orientation at impact, and tissue elasticity, with the height of the fall being the primary factor.
He added that while the examination of clothing is an integral part of any forensic investigation, no clothing was available for review in this case.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Home Ministry staff warned over social media posts during WFH periods
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: Home Ministry staff have been reminded against posting social media content that suggests they are "slacking off" during work-from-home arrangements.
Home Ministry secretary-general Datuk Dr Awang Alik Jeman said the ministry would not hesitate to take disciplinary action against those found portraying a lifestyle of truancy while on duty.
"For those granted WFH, please do not upload photos or posts on social media that can be construed as playing truant.
"Avoid posting pictures of yourselves playing badminton or visiting shopping complexes during working hours.
"Please refrain from sharing such content on Facebook or any other social media platforms," he said.
He said this during the Home Ministry's monthly assembly here today, following the government's announcement of work-from-home arrangements to help mitigate economic pressures arising from the Middle East conflict.
Awang Alik said the reminder was intended to safeguard staff careers and uphold discipline within the ministry.
He stressed that WFH was a privilege meant to sustain productivity and must not be misused for personal leisure that could damage the ministry's reputation.
"If this happens, I am telling you frankly: action will be taken.
"I am advising you early — do not do things that will harm your own positions.
"We will remain firm in taking action and there will be no exceptions," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Stronger scrap metal oversight needed to curb MRT cable theft [WATCH]
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Stronger oversight of scrap metal transactions is crucial to curb Mass Rapid Transit (MRT) cable theft and other infrastructure-related crimes, says Alliance for a Safe Community chairman Tan Sri Lee Lam Thye.
He said greater transparency and accountability within the recycling supply chain would help disrupt the monetisation of stolen materials.
He said such measures must form part of a broader, intelligence-led strategy to dismantle organised networks behind cable theft, rather than focusing solely on those directly involved in stealing infrastructure components.
"We cannot solve today's crimes with yesterday's thinking. When the same problem persists despite repeated effort, it is not the effort that is lacking, but the approach," he told the New Straits Times.
Lee called for closer collaboration between policymakers, enforcement agencies, industry players and the public, adding that Malaysia had the capability to address the issue through coordinated and sustained action.
His remarks come amid continued cable theft incidents disrupting MRT services in the Klang Valley, despite heightened security measures.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Japan's Nikkei falls as US-Iran peace talks falter
Article:
TOKYO: Japan's Nikkei share average slipped one per cent on Monday as the collapse of US-Iran peace talks and reports of a US Navy plan to block the Strait of Hormuz cast fresh doubts on the durability of an ongoing ceasefire.
The Nikkei was down one per cent at 56,357.40, as of midday break, after posting its steepest weekly gain in more than a year last week. The broader Topix slid 0.5 per cent in choppy trading to 3,721.78.
US President Donald Trump said on Sunday the US Navy would start blocking the Strait of Hormuz, a choke point for 20 per cent of the world's daily energy supplies that Iran effectively closed since the war started in late February. The announcement drove oil prices to jump above US$100 a barrel in early trade on Monday.
"I don't think there are that many investors who expected everything to be agreed (at the weekend talks) and for everything to go smoothly," said Shuutaro Yasuda, market analyst at Tokai Tokyo Intelligence Laboratory.
"That said, it's obviously not good news, so stocks are falling. But I think the reason we're not seeing a really extreme risk-off move is exactly that."
Across the Tokyo Stock Exchange's 33 industry sub-indexes, energy exploration stocks stood out with a 3.2 per cent gain. In contrast, electric power and gas and air transport each lost 1.8 per cent.
Market breadth was negative, with 162 stocks falling versus 62 advancing in the Nikkei, led by losses in semiconductor-related stocks.
Ibiden, a chip packaging and electronics firm, recorded the steepest drop among blue chips, falling 4.3 per cent. Tokyo Electron, a major semiconductor equipment maker, also weighed heavily in the index, declining 3.7 per cent.
Still, solid corporate earnings helped limit the decline in market sentiment, said Hiroshi Namioka, chief strategist and fund manager at T&D Asset Management.
Yaskawa Electric, an industrial robots and motion control systems maker, was among the Nikkei's biggest percentage gainers, up 5.7 per cent. Ryohin Keikaku, operator of MUJI brand, rose as much as 4.6 per cent before paring gains to 1.3 per cent.
"Although the overall market is paying attention to earnings, when it comes to sector selection, there's no doubt that the moves are being driven by expectations of crude oil prices," Namioka said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Oil leaps, dollar firm and stocks wobble as US-Iran peace talks collapse
Article:
SINGAPORE/LONDON: Oil prices surged on Monday as the US moved to impose a blockade on Iranian shipping after the collapse of weekend peace talks, while the dollar rose and stocks and bonds fell in Asia ahead of the start of US earnings season later in the day.
The US move, aimed at putting pressure on Tehran, leaves a fragile ceasefire hanging in the balance and no end in sight to the choke on Middle East energy exports - though the mood on trading floors leaned toward hoping for a resolution.
Brent crude futures were up 7.3 per cent at US$102 a barrel. S&P 500 futures were down 0.7 per cent through the Asia morning and European futures fell 1.3 per cent.
US Treasuries and bonds around Asia traded lower, with Japan's benchmark 10-year yield hitting a 29-year high of 2.49 per cent, though moves were relatively modest and took most assets to roughly where they sat before last week's ceasefire.
"The market is now largely back to conditions before the ceasefire, except now the US will block the remaining up to (two million barrels) Iranian-linked flows through the Strait of Hormuz as well," said MST Marquee analyst Saul Kavonic.
"The key remaining question is if the US renews strikes on Iran, raising the risk of strikes on energy infrastructure across the region which could have a further lasting impact beyond the duration of the war."
The Wall Street Journal reported Trump and his advisers were weighing limited strikes on Iran, though there were no immediate reports of attacks in the Asia day.
Trump said on Sunday that the price of oil and gasoline ​may remain high into the midterm elections in the US in November, a rare acknowledgement of the potential political fallout from the war.
DOLLAR HIGHER
The euro fell about 0.3 per cent to US$1.1687 and risk-sensitive currencies such as the Australian dollar slipped a little further.
Equity benchmarks from Hong Kong to Tokyo, Seoul and Sydney dropped around one per cent and MSCI's broadest index of Asian shares outside Japan fell one per cent.
"The market is saying that it doesn't believe Trump is going to strike more military assets or take over the Strait of Hormuz," said Russel Chesler, head of investments and capital markets at VanEck in Sydney.
However, he added, there were growing worries about inflation, which would intensify the longer the oil shock lasts.
"Even if the Strait (of Hormuz) is to reopen the flow of oil is going to be pretty slow so we're going to be stuck with high prices for some time," he said.
With inflation fears reviving, investors are now bracing for central banks, such as the European Central Bank and Bank of England, tilting towards raising rates in a sharp reversal from pre-war bets on rate cuts or a prolonged pause.
In emerging markets the Hungarian forint was up sharply, scaling multi-year highs on the dollar and euro, after Hungary's veteran nationalist leader Viktor Orban lost power to an upstart centre-right coalition at Sunday's election.
The result is likely to pave the way for European Union funding to flow to Hungary and Ukraine.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Escaped Pakistani detainee case: Police probe SOP lapses, weaknesses
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, April 13 — Police have opened an investigation paper to identify any weaknesses or non-compliance with standard operating procedures (SOP) following the escape of a Pakistani man from Sungai Buloh Prison in an incident last Thursday.
Selangor police chief Datuk Shazeli Kahar said the investigation is being conducted under Sections 223 and 224 of the Penal Code,
Berita Harian
reported.
“Police have opened an investigation paper to ensure any weaknesses or non-compliance with SOP are examined under Sections 223 and 224 of the Penal Code.
“We will look into any negligence or weaknesses, and appropriate action will be taken,” he said at a press conference at the Selangor police headquarters here today.
He said efforts to track down the suspect are ongoing, and police believe, based on information received, that the detainee is still in the Sungai Buloh area.
He added that statements have been recorded from various parties, including police officers, Prison Department officials, and detainees who witnessed the incident.
“We also believe the suspect has not contacted any associates and is currently alone,” he said.
Previously, Sungai Buloh district police chief Supt Mohd Hafiz Muhammad Nor said the incident occurred at about 6pm last Thursday during the transfer of remand detainees to Sungai Buloh Prison by the Shah Alam Escort Unit.
Upon arrival at the prison parade ground, the suspect escaped while alighting from the police vehicle.
On the day of the incident, the remand detainee had just completed court proceedings at the Shah Alam Court involving sexual offences against children, robbery, and immigration offences.
The suspect has been identified as Muhammad Hassan, 24, and was last seen wearing an orange T-shirt and black trousers.

Title: Court grants FashionValet founder Vivy Yusof, husband temporary release of passport to perform Haj, trial postponed to July
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR,  April 13 — The trial involving FashionValet Sdn Bhd (FashionValet) founder Datin Vivy Yusof and her husband,  Datuk Fadzarudin Shah Anuar, for criminal breach of trust (CBT) involving RM8 million in investment funds, which was supposed to begin today, has been postponed to July.
Sessions Court Judge Rosli Ahmad postponed the trial following an application by the couple’s counsel, Shaarvin Raaj, to vacate the earlier trial dates scheduled today, April 14 to 17, May 18 to 22 and June 8 to 12, 2026, as they need to prepare their defence statements.
Following this, the court set July 20 and 24, August 17 and 18, October 16 and 23, November 3, 20 and 30, December 17 and 18 this year, as well as January 15, 28 and 29 as the new dates for the hearing.
Earlier, Shaarvin informed the court that documents related to the case needed to be submitted to auditors, a process expected to take about six weeks before the defence could finalise and submit its statement to the prosecution.
Deputy Public Prosecutor (DPP) Noorsyazwani Mohamad Sobry, assisted by DPP Elyza Amira Habeeb Rahman, did not object to the postponement.
During the same proceedings, the court also allowed the couple’s application for temporary release of their passports to perform the Haj pilgrimage, and also an order for the documents to be returned to the court by June 18.
On December 5, 2024, Vivy, whose full name is Vivy Sofinas Yusof, 38, and Fadzarudin, 37, pleaded not guilty to charges of CBT involving funds from Khazanah Nasional Berhad and Permodalan Nasional Berhad amounting to RM8 million.
The couple, as directors of FashionValet Sdn Bhd, were accused of committing CBT by authorising a payment of RM8 million from FashionValet’s corporate bank account to 30 Maple Sdn Bhd without the approval of the company’s Board of Directors.
The offence was allegedly committed on August 21, 2018, at Public Bank Berhad, Bukit Damansara branch.
They are charged under Section 409 of the Penal Code, read together with Section 34, which provides for a maximum jail term of 20 years, whipping, and a possible fine upon conviction.
They also pleaded not guilty to an alternative charge of dishonest misappropriation of property belonging to FashionValet, namely Khazanah and PNB investment funds amounting to RM8 million, at the same location and date.
The charge,  under Section 403 of the Penal Code, carries a prison sentence of not less than six months and a maximum of five years with whipping and shall be fined, if convicted. — Bernama

Title: E-hailing ride turns drug run: Johor cops arrest mule in Pontian bust
Article:
PONTIAN, April 13 — Police have uncovered a drug distribution tactic involving the use of e-hailing services following the arrest of a drug mule during an operation last Friday.
Pontian police chief Supt Hadzrat Hussien Mion Hussain said a team from the District Narcotics Criminal Investigation Division (BSJND), conducting an Ops Tapis patrol, saw a suspicious e-hailing car by the roadside along Jalan Kukup, Kampung Penerok, at about 9.15pm and approached it.
Following an inspection, the team found crystal-like powder, believed to be syabu, weighing about 1,528 grammes and valued at RM69,080.
“A 36-year-old unemployed local man, who is a passenger in the car, was arrested to facilitate the investigation,” he said in a statement today.
Hadzrat Hussien said the suspect admitted to accepting an assignment from a man he had befriended while in the Simpang Renggam Prison in 2020.
He said that according to the suspect, he had been instructed over the phone by the man to pick up and deliver the drug supply to a specified location for a fee of RM1,000.
The suspect, who has two drug and criminal records, had received instructions to pick up the drug supply that was placed in a blue Perodua Bezza car in a parking area near Tea Garden, Perling, and he then used the Grab service to go to the given location, he added.
The police have obtained obtained and order to remand the suspect under Section 117 of the Criminal Procedure Code for seven days until April 17 to facilitate investigations under Section 39B of the Dangerous Drugs Act 1952.
Meanwhile, the e-hailing driver, a 43-year-old local woman, has been released on police bail.
Police also reminded the public not to accept delivery jobs involving unknown contents or be lured by attractive payments, as such actions could result in becoming an unwitting drug mule. — Bernama

Title: ‘Even burger stalls look better’: Home minister orders upgrades for Malaysia’s border posts
Article:
PUTRAJAYA, April 13 — Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail today instructed several Malaysia’s border posts to be upgraded as some are currently in a state that is less presentable than even a burger stall.
He said he personally inspected several border checkpoints yesterday in Kedah and Perak, including Padang Besar, Bukit Kayu Hitam, Durian Burung and Pengkalan Hulu, and found that some duty posts used by security forces were in poor condition.
“I cannot stand it when I see the duty posts of our officers on the ground, their shelter looks like a burger stall. Even a burger stall looks nicer,” he said in his speech during his ministry’s monthly assembly.
Saifuddin said he had instructed the Home Ministry’s secretary-general Datuk Awang Alik Jeman to urgently upgrade the facilities, particularly at Pengkalan Hulu, adding that only a small number of units were needed to improve the situation.
He said the upgrades should not require significant expenditure and could be carried out immediately to ensure a more conducive working environment for frontline officers.
“I told the secretary-general, it is not a big number, just two or three units. We should improve them. We did the same for the Wang Kelian post. The cost is not that big. We will expedite it,” he said.
Saifuddin said the improvements were important as they reflected the government’s commitment to frontline personnel tasked with preventing cross-border smuggling and monitoring vehicle movement at remote posts.
“We want to show that our leadership in home ministry cares, and even small things like this we resolve them,” he said.

Title: Don’t panic, don’t relax: Home minister urges Malaysians to brace for possible energy-driven shocks
Article:
PUTRAJAYA, April 13 — Malaysians should prepare for possible economic and supply disruptions if the ongoing geopolitical conflict in West Asia continues to affect global energy routes, Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said today.
He said the Strait of Hormuz remains a critical shipping lane for crude oil bound for Port Dickson, Melaka and Pengerang, which together account for about 60 per cent of Malaysia’s downstream needs in gas, petrol and diesel.
“If the conflict continues, we must anticipate more difficult conditions. The challenge for the government, particularly in terms of communication strategy, is to prepare the public to accept the reality. The pain is coming.
“But we do not want the public to panic. At the same time, we also do not want them to become complacent. So how do we balance this? How do we manage it? We must find a way. We need to think carefully about how to do this. We do not want panic, but there must be no denial syndrome,” he said during the Home Ministry monthly assembly.
Saifuddin Nasution said the government’s priority remains protecting public welfare and ensuring Malaysians do not immediately feel the full impact of external shocks.
He pointed to risks such as rising prices and possible supply disruptions, while stressing that maintaining economic stability remains a key focus of the administration.
He said Putrajaya will continue its “transparent” approach by openly communicating supply pressures and price risks to the public, while using subsidies as a buffer against sudden cost increases.
“So our approach is one, transparent. We share information openly. We say clearly that we are facing supply issues, for example, or we are facing challenges of rising prices,” he said.
Saifuddin Nasution also highlighted the fiscal burden of subsidy support, noting that under current arrangements – including targeted fuel subsidies and diesel assistance schemes – government spending had previously been around RM700 million a month.
However, he said this has now surged to about RM6 billion a month due to broader subsidy obligations.
“From a fiscal perspective, this is very burdensome,” he said, adding that despite the pressure, the government will continue prioritising public welfare.
He said Putrajaya is still able to meet subsidy commitments for now, but acknowledged growing strain on public finances if global tensions persist.

Title: Fewer people buying new cars after Aidilfitri as global uncertainty dents confidence, says industry group
Article:
CYBERJAYA, April 13 — The ongoing conflict in West Asia is expected to weigh on Malaysia’s automotive industry as demand for new vehicles shows a slight dip, according to industry players.
Malaysian Malay Vehicle Importers and Traders Association (Pekema) president Datuk Mohamed Nazari Noordin said the softer demand trend was first observed after the Hari Raya Aidilfitri period and has been influenced by global geopolitical uncertainty, Harian Metro reported today.
“There is a slight drop in demand for new cars, especially after Hari Raya, and the West Asia conflict has also affected buyer confidence,” he said.
“Most people are taking a wait-and-see approach and are being cautious in making purchases,” he was quoted telling reporters at Wisma Yayasan Pekema.
Nazari said consumers are now prioritising essential spending over big-ticket commitments such as vehicle or property purchases.
However, he said vehicle prices remain stable for now as most imported vehicles handled by Pekema come from Japan, with currency exchange rates still relatively favourable against the ringgit.
He said the main challenge currently facing the industry lies in logistics and supply chain disruptions, including congestion at local ports.
“Recently, about 1,500 vehicles had to be unloaded at ports in the Philippines because they could not be processed at Port Klang due to congestion,” he was quoted as saying.
Nazari warned that if the West Asia conflict continues, shipping costs could rise and eventually place upward pressure on vehicle prices.
On market outlook, he said Pekema does not expect a sharp decline in sales, but anticipates a flat performance compared to last year, with a slight dip possible if global conditions remain unstable.
“Sales performance for Dongfeng electric vehicles last year was encouraging, with about 300 units sold, but current trends show a wait-and-see approach among buyers,” he was quoted as saying.

Title: Unpaid sales tax lands two in court: Penang directors face charges over RM16,000 in arrears
Article:
GEORGE TOWN, April 13 — Two directors of a company involved in the manufacturing and marketing of prayer items were charged at the Sessions Court today with 18 counts of failing to pay sales tax amounting to more than RM16,000.
Moganadass Virachami, 45, and his mother, Saroja Devi Subramaniam, 65, pleaded not guilty and claimed trial after the charges were read out before Judge Irwan Suainbon.
According to the charges, both individuals, as directors of KVS Industries Sdn Bhd, are accused of failing to pay sales tax for 18 taxable periods between March 1, 2021 and December 31, 2023, involving total arrears and penalties of RM16,145.44.
According to the charges, Moganadass and Saroja Devi, who are directors of KVS Industries Sdn Bhd, are accused of failing to pay sales tax for 18 taxable periods between March 1, 2021 and December 31, 2023, involving a total arrears of sales tax and penalties of RM16,145.44.
The offences were allegedly committed at the Registration and Revenue Management Enforcement Branch Office at the Royal Malaysian Customs Department Complex in Seberang Jaya between June 1, 2021 and February 1, 2024.
They were charged under Section 26(5) of the Sales Tax Act 2018 (Act 806), read together with Section 26(9) of the same Act, which carries a maximum fine of RM50,000, imprisonment of up to three years, or both upon conviction.
The prosecution is conducted by the Royal Customs Department prosecuting officer Karthy Gasedev Deva Rajoo, while both accused are unrepresented.
The court fixed May 18 for mention to allow for the submission of documents and the appointment of legal counsel. — Bernama

Title: Kelana Jaya LRT line fully operational after early morning track switch disruption at Universiti station
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, April 13 — Train services on the Kelana Jaya LRT line have resumed normal operations following an early morning disruption caused by a track switch fault at Universiti station today.
Rapid Rail Sdn Bhd said full services were restored at 7.21am after initial repair works were completed by its engineering team.
The disruption, which occurred at about 5.57am, had affected train schedules along the line, prompting the activation of alternative train services to help passengers continue their journeys.
At around 7.10am, engineering teams completed initial rectification works and conducted system and track component tests to ensure operations met required safety standards before services were resumed.
“However, engineering and operations teams will continue to monitor train movements throughout operating hours to ensure smooth service on the Kelana Jaya line,” Rapid Rail said in a statement.
As a precautionary measure, auxiliary police officers and station staff remain stationed at platforms and concourse areas to assist in managing passenger flow at affected stations.
Rapid Rail apologised for the inconvenience caused and advised passengers to refer to its official social media channels for the latest service updates.

Title: Busted at sea: Two tankers held over illicit diesel transfer off Penang
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, April 13 — Malaysia’s Maritime Enforcement Agency (MMEA) said it has detained two tankers for allegedly conducting an illegal ship-to-ship transfer of about 700,000 litres of diesel off the island of Penang over the weekend.
Malaysia has been cracking down on fuel smuggling amid mounting shortages and supply disruptions throughout the region caused by the war in the Middle East.
Penang MMEA director Muhammad Suffi Mohd Ramli said authorities carried out checks following an intelligence tip about the ships, which were anchored in the waters off Bagan Ajam on Saturday.
“The inspection found that both ships were in a coupled state and were suspected of carrying out ship-to-ship oil transfer activities without permission,” Muhammad Suffi said in a statement on Sunday.
The alleged transfer involved about 700,000 litres of Euro 5 diesel, while the total amount seized was estimated at around 800,000 litres, with a value of about RM5.43 million (US$1.37 million), he said.
Authorities also arrested 22 crew members, comprising Malaysian, Myanmar, Russian, Philippine and Indonesian nationals.
The MMEA did not specify the origin of the tankers, the diesel, or the fuel’s destination.
The waters off Malaysia are known to be a regular site for illegal ship-to-ship transfers, where oil is shifted between tankers at sea to obscure its origin. Malaysian authorities said in July last year that they would enforce related rules with more rigour.
